name: Rose Murphy
abstract: Rose Murphy (April 28, 1913 – November 16, 1989) was an American jazz pianist and singer, famous for the song "Busy Line" and her unique vocal style.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Café Society

works.t: Porgy and Bess: Act I, Scene 1. Summertime
I Can't Give You Anything But Love
I Can't Give You Anything But Love

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I said, 'Come down, 'cause Rose Murphy is ill and she won't be able to show up.' So Rose Murphy came in and after Rose Murphy we had Gladys Palmer as intermission pianist, in 1938.

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: He was Rose Murphy's first husband... I don't remember Pickens first name, but he was a tall, lanky guy and he was a marvelous piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: And they had another piano player around there named Pickett, he married the chi-chi girl, Rose Murphy.

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: we played at the Swanee Inn with Shelton Brooks, Junior, and I and Rose Murphy the Chi Chi.

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: On the piano I like Mary Lou and Connie Berry and Nellie Lutcher and Rose Murphy.

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: And the piano player was Rose Murphy. And I’d see her playing...
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002
